title: neogaillonia afghanica
side_effects: Neogaillonia afghanica can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Ingesting its leaves or extracts can cause nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, while prolonged exposure to its pollen can lead to headaches and fatigue. Prolonged use of Neogaillonia afghanica extracts in medicinal products has also been linked to liver and kidney damage in some individuals. Furthermore, improper handling of the plant can cause skin rashes, blisters, and itching due to its toxic sap.
